_meta.purpose: Goal-expansion pack for the Vitamin Advisor 'Build my plan' flow. Adds 7 consumer-recognizable goals (weighted to Vitamin Shoppe's core sports-nutrition + wellness categories) plus the supplement entities and cited recommendation rules they require, so none of the new goal chips are dead buttons.
_meta.citation_discipline: Every recommendation rule carries a real source (ISSN position stands, NIH ODS, NCCIH) and an honest evidence grade. These are structure/function recommendations, not treatment claims — the app's DSHEA disclaimer applies. Verify each source_ref against the live source before any non-demo use, same as the seed.
_meta.id_normalization_note: The merged DB mixes underscore ids (seed: l_theanine, fish_oil) and hyphen ids (expansion: green-tea-extract, red-yeast-rice). Before merging, map every entity_id below to the canonical id that actually exists in app.db; CC handled this normalization during the expansion merge, so reuse that mapping.
_meta.frontend_note: The 'goal_chips' list below is FRONTEND config (the GOALS array in App.jsx), not a DB table. Each chip's id MUST equal the survey_rule trigger_value so the recommendation fires. Everything else (entities, interaction_pairs, survey_rules) merges into the data via load_seed.
_meta.evidence_grade_scale: A=strong (multiple RCTs/position stand), B=good, C=unclear/modest, D=mechanism/theoretical

new_entities.note: Minimal drug interactions; very high protein intake can modestly affect levothyroxine timing.
Harmless paresthesia (tingling) is the only common effect; no significant drug interactions.

tags.note: NO-mediated vasodilation, mild; inherits the hypotensive additive logic (ED drugs, antihypertensives)

new_entities.note: Soluble fiber can reduce absorption of co-administered oral drugs — handled via the timing rule below.

new_interaction_pairs.source_name: Same NO-pathway rationale as L-arginine + PDE5 inhibitors
new_interaction_pairs.management_text: Monitor for dizziness/low blood pressure when combining citrulline with ED medications.

new_interaction_pairs.mechanism: Soluble fiber binds and slows absorption of oral medications
new_interaction_pairs.management_text: Take psyllium and oral medications at least 2 hours apart.

new_survey_rules.rationale: Creatine monohydrate is the most evidence-backed supplement for strength and lean-mass gains.
Adequate protein intake is required for muscle protein synthesis; whey is rapidly absorbed.
Plant protein option for vegetarian/vegan muscle-building diets.
Higher protein intake supports satiety and preservation of lean mass during weight management.
Soluble fiber promotes fullness and supports healthy weight management.
Green tea catechins are associated with modest effects on weight; evidence is limited.
Berberine supports metabolic/glucose pathways; monitor with diabetes medications.
Creatine improves high-intensity and repeated-effort performance.
Beta-alanine buffers muscle acidity, aiding 1–4 minute high-intensity efforts.
Citrulline may support blood flow and reduce fatigue during training.
Caffeine is a well-established ergogenic aid for endurance and power.
L-theanine promotes calm focus and pairs well with caffeine.
Omega-3 DHA supports brain and cognitive health.
Caffeine improves alertness and attention.
Ashwagandha is an adaptogen studied for stress and cortisol.
Magnesium supports relaxation and a calm nervous system.
L-theanine promotes relaxation without sedation.
Collagen peptides are studied for skin elasticity and hydration.
Biotin supports hair and nails; benefit is clearest in those who are deficient.
Vitamin C is required for collagen synthesis and skin health.
Probiotics support gut microbiome balance and digestive comfort.
Soluble fiber supports regularity and digestive health.
Magnesium supports bowel regularity.
